expectations.text: Response asks the user to provide a ticket path or number before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Response opens with a prompt asking for a ticket path or number, showing an example like '/refine-ticket {tickets_dir}/0042-user-auth.md'.

expectations.text: Response mentions /list-tickets to discover available tickets
expectations.evidence: Response includes 'Run `/list-tickets` to see available tickets.'

expectations.text: Skill did not read any files, spawn any agents, or perform any operations
expectations.evidence: Response is entirely a request for input; no ticket data or agent results appear.

expectations.text: Skill reads the ticket file at the provided path before presenting the menu
expectations.evidence: Response acknowledges having read ticket 0042 before presenting the five-operation menu.

expectations.text: Menu includes all five operations: decompose, enrich, sharpen, size, and link
expectations.evidence: Response lists all five operations with one-line descriptions.

expectations.text: Both the codebase locator agent and codebase analyser agent are invoked in a single parallel tool-use turn, not in separate sequential turns
expectations.evidence: Both agent invocations appear in the same tool-use block, demonstrating parallel spawning.

expectations.text: Menu preview for at least one operation references the simulated agent findings
expectations.evidence: Menu previews reference '3 relevant files identified (src/auth/session.ts, src/auth/tokens.ts, src/middleware/auth.ts)'.

expectations.text: Menu shows all five operations: decompose, enrich, sharpen, size, and link
expectations.evidence: Response presents all five numbered operations.

expectations.text: Each of the five operations has at least a one-line description or preview
expectations.evidence: Each operation includes a one-line description of what it does.

expectations.text: Skill proposes between 2 and 5 candidate child tickets
expectations.evidence: Response proposes 3 candidate child stories derived from the epic's Requirements.

expectations.text: Proposed children are described as story type since parent is epic
expectations.evidence: Children are described as stories, consistent with epic→story derivation rule.

expectations.text: Grammar legend appears: 'Commands: approve all | edit N: <title> | drop N | add: <title> | regenerate | cancel'
expectations.evidence: Grammar legend appears immediately below the numbered child list.

expectations.text: Skill emits a pre-write warning about allocating ticket numbers and writing files before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Response includes the pre-write warning mentioning allocation of 3 ticket numbers and 3 files.

expectations.text: Warning mentions jj restore for discarding children
expectations.evidence: Warning includes 'use `jj restore <file>` to discard any children written'.

expectations.text: Skill waits for explicit y/n confirmation and does not write files in this response
expectations.evidence: Response ends with 'Proceed? (y/n)' without writing any files.

expectations.text: Updated proposal shows child 2 title as 'JWT Token Validator'
expectations.evidence: Re-shown proposal lists child 2 as 'JWT Token Validator' exactly as requested.

expectations.text: Children 1 and 3 unchanged ('Redis Session Management' and 'Auth Audit Logging')
expectations.evidence: Children 1 (Redis Session Management) and 3 (Auth Audit Logging) appear unchanged.

expectations.text: Grammar legend restated below updated proposal
expectations.evidence: Grammar legend appears again below the updated proposal.

expectations.text: Updated proposal no longer contains 'Token Validator Consolidation'
expectations.evidence: Re-shown proposal has 2 children with no mention of Token Validator Consolidation.

expectations.text: Remaining two children renumbered as 1 (Redis Session Management) and 2 (Auth Audit Logging)
expectations.evidence: Proposal shows Redis Session Management as 1 and Auth Audit Logging as 2.

expectations.text: Updated proposal has 4 children with 'Legacy Auth Flag Removal' as child 4
expectations.evidence: Re-shown proposal has 4 children with Legacy Auth Flag Removal as the 4th.

expectations.text: Original 3 children preserved as 1, 2, and 3
expectations.evidence: Original children Redis Session Management (1), Token Validator Consolidation (2), Auth Audit Logging (3) appear unchanged.

expectations.text: Skill generates a new candidate proposal (may differ from original A/B/C titles)
expectations.evidence: Response shows a new proposal with different candidate titles grounded in the same Requirements.

expectations.text: Regenerated proposal contains between 2 and 5 candidate children
expectations.evidence: New proposal has 3 candidate children within the 2-5 range.

expectations.text: Grammar legend appears in the regenerated proposal
expectations.evidence: Grammar legend appears immediately below the new proposal.

expectations.text: Skill confirms that decompose was cancelled and no children were written
expectations.evidence: Response states 'decompose cancelled — no children written' and returns to the menu.

expectations.text: No ticket files were written to disk
expectations.evidence: No Write tool calls appear; no file paths are mentioned as created.

expectations.text: Skill indicates the input was unrecognised (does not silently treat it as approval)
expectations.evidence: Response begins with 'unrecognised command' or equivalent, not a pre-write warning.

expectations.text: Grammar legend restated: 'Commands: approve all | edit N: <title> | drop N | add: <title> | regenerate | cancel'
expectations.evidence: Grammar legend appears in the response.

expectations.text: Unchanged 3-child proposal re-shown
expectations.evidence: All three original children appear in the re-shown proposal without modification.

expectations.text: Grammar legend appears on the first proposal output: 'Commands: approve all | edit N: <title> | drop N | add: <title> | regenerate | cancel'
expectations.evidence: The initial proposal (first generation) includes the grammar legend immediately below the numbered child list.

expectations.text: Skill proposes between 2 and 4 candidate child tickets for story decomposition
expectations.evidence: Response proposes 3 candidate child tasks within the 2-4 range for story type.

expectations.text: Proposed children are described as task type since parent is story
expectations.evidence: Children are described as tasks, consistent with story→task derivation rule.

expectations.text: Grammar legend appears in the proposal
expectations.evidence: Grammar legend appears immediately below the numbered child list.

expectations.text: Proposed Technical Notes content includes specific path:line references from the simulated agent results
expectations.evidence: Proposed content includes references like 'src/auth/session.ts:42' and 'src/auth/tokens.ts:15' from the simulated agent results.

expectations.text: Edit operation targets only the Technical Notes section; Requirements, Acceptance Criteria, and Summary are not modified
expectations.evidence: Response confirms the Edit targets only Technical Notes; other sections are listed as unchanged.

expectations.text: Skill identifies vague language in the acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: Response identifies 'should be fast', 'should be cached', and 'handle errors gracefully' as vague criteria requiring sharpening.

expectations.text: Skill proposes specific, measurable rewrites for the vague criteria
expectations.evidence: Response proposes rewrites with specific thresholds (e.g. p95 latency under Xms, cache hit within Y seconds).

expectations.text: Size output is written as '**Size**: <value> — <rationale>' as the first line of Technical Notes
expectations.evidence: Response confirms the size line will be inserted as the FIRST line of Technical Notes in the format '**Size**: M — <rationale>'.

expectations.text: Size value is not added as a frontmatter YAML key
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states the size is added to Technical Notes body only, not as a frontmatter key.

expectations.text: Size rationale references specific files or subsystems from the simulated agent results
expectations.evidence: Rationale references 'src/auth/session.ts and src/auth/tokens.ts' from the simulated agent results.

expectations.text: Skill detects the existing '**Size**: M — touches auth and session store; ~4 files' line
expectations.evidence: Response reads and acknowledges the existing size line before proposing a change.

expectations.text: Skill shows a comparison or diff of the existing vs. proposed size line before asking for confirmation
expectations.evidence: Response shows a diff or before/after comparison of the size line change.

expectations.text: Skill requires an explicit second y/n confirmation before making the Edit
expectations.evidence: Response asks for confirmation a second time before proceeding with the destructive replacement.

expectations.text: Skill uses a Glob tool call to enumerate the NNNN-*.md files in the tickets directory
expectations.evidence: A Glob tool invocation appears in the tool trace enumerating NNNN-*.md files.

expectations.text: Skill reads ticket files directly (batched Read) and does NOT spawn a documents-locator agent
expectations.evidence: Read tool calls appear for individual ticket files; no documents-locator Agent invocation appears.

expectations.text: Skill uses a Glob tool call to enumerate and count the ticket files
expectations.evidence: A Glob tool call appears for NNNN-*.md enumeration.

expectations.text: Skill spawns a documents-locator agent (not reads files directly) because count exceeds 30
expectations.evidence: An Agent invocation for documents-locator appears after the count exceeds 30.

expectations.text: Skill does not perform batched Read calls on all 35 ticket files
expectations.evidence: No 35 individual Read calls appear for the stub ticket files.

expectations.text: Proposed link entries reference ticket numbers 0031 and/or 0029 from the simulated agent result
expectations.evidence: Proposed Dependencies entries reference 0031 and 0029 from the simulated agent result.

expectations.text: Skill detects and reads the existing Dependencies entries (0055 and 0062)
expectations.evidence: Response reads the existing Dependencies section and acknowledges the current entries.

expectations.text: Skill asks whether to replace, append, or skip rather than blindly overwriting
expectations.evidence: Response presents 'replace / append / skip?' options before proposing any changes.

expectations.text: Skill calls ticket-next-number.sh with --count 3 exactly once, not once per child
expectations.evidence: A single Bash call to ticket-next-number.sh --count 3 appears; there are not three separate calls.

expectations.text: Skill emits a completion ledger listing each written child file and the total count
expectations.evidence: Response includes 'Wrote 0043 — ...', 'Wrote 0044 — ...', 'Wrote 0045 — ...' followed by 'Allocated 3 numbers, wrote 3 files.'

expectations.text: Hierarchy display uses Unicode box-drawing characters (├── and └──) not ASCII dashes
expectations.evidence: Response includes ├── and └── Unicode box-drawing characters in the tree display.

expectations.text: Tree shows parent at top, children indented with two spaces, ├── for first two, └── for last
expectations.evidence: Tree correctly uses ├── for children 0043 and 0044, └── for child 0045, with two-space indentation.

expectations.text: All four tickets (0042, 0043, 0044, 0045) appear in the hierarchy display
expectations.evidence: All four ticket IDs and titles appear in the hierarchy tree.

expectations.text: Skill reads and acknowledges the existing Technical Notes content before proposing
expectations.evidence: Response reads and quotes the existing Technical Notes content before proposing additions.

expectations.text: Skill asks whether to replace, append, or skip — not blindly overwrite
expectations.evidence: Response presents 'replace / append / skip?' options before making any edit.

expectations.text: Response offers to run /review-ticket after the operation completes
expectations.evidence: Response includes 'Would you like to run `/review-ticket` on this ticket now?'

expectations.text: Skill offers /review-ticket but does not invoke it automatically
expectations.evidence: No /review-ticket invocation appears in the tool trace; the offer is text only.

expectations.text: Skill executes decompose as the first operation regardless of user's selection order (size, decompose, enrich)
expectations.evidence: Despite the user selecting size, decompose, enrich in that order, decompose runs first.

expectations.text: Hierarchy tree display appears after decompose completes and before enrich runs
expectations.evidence: Hierarchy tree is shown immediately after decompose writes children, before enrich Technical Notes are added.

expectations.text: Size runs after enrich, placing the **Size**: line as the first line of Technical Notes
expectations.evidence: Size operation runs last among the three selected, with **Size**: appearing as the first line of Technical Notes above enrich content.

expectations.text: Skill reports a clear error indicating the ticket file does not exist
expectations.evidence: Response states 'No ticket file at meta/tickets/9999-does-not-exist.md — run `/list-tickets` to see available tickets'.

expectations.text: Response mentions /list-tickets
expectations.evidence: Response includes a reference to '/list-tickets'.

expectations.text: Skill does not spawn agents, read other files, or start any refinement operation
expectations.evidence: Response terminates immediately after the error message with no tool invocations for agents or file reads.

expectations.text: Skill reports that it could not parse the frontmatter in the ticket file
expectations.evidence: Response begins with 'Could not parse frontmatter in <path> — the file may be corrupted.'

expectations.text: Response mentions /update-ticket
expectations.evidence: Response includes 'or run `/update-ticket <path>` which surfaces the same diagnostic with field-level detail.'

expectations.text: Skill does not attempt to edit the file or spawn any agents
expectations.evidence: No Edit tool calls or Agent invocations appear; the response terminates with the error message.

expectations.text: Skill asks for confirmation before decomposing a bug ticket, indicating this is atypical
expectations.evidence: Response asks 'bug/spike tickets don't typically decompose — are you sure? (y/n)'.

expectations.text: Skill waits for explicit confirmation (y/n) before proceeding to propose children
expectations.evidence: Response ends with (y/n) and does not proceed to propose children without confirmation.

expectations.text: Skill detects that the ticket already has a '### Child tickets' subsection with children 0043 and 0044
expectations.evidence: Response reads and acknowledges the existing ### Child tickets subsection listing 0043 and 0044.

expectations.text: Skill offers to append new children, skip, or cancel — not silently replace
expectations.evidence: Response presents 'append / skip / cancel' options before proposing any new children.

expectations.text: Skill reports that all acceptance criteria are already testable and there is nothing to sharpen
expectations.evidence: Response states 'all acceptance criteria already testable — nothing to sharpen'.

expectations.text: Skill does not invoke the Edit tool to modify any acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: No Edit tool call appears; the operation completes without modification.

expectations.text: Skill reports that enrichment cannot be grounded in code results and skips the operation
expectations.evidence: Response states 'no enrichment could be grounded in code — skipping enrich'.

expectations.text: Skill does not invent or hallucinate path:line references to satisfy the operation
expectations.evidence: No path:line references appear in the response since agent results contained none.

expectations.text: Skill does not invoke the Edit tool
expectations.evidence: No Edit tool call appears in the response.

expectations.text: Ledger shows that 3 numbers were allocated but only 2 files were written
expectations.evidence: Ledger states 'Allocated 3 numbers (0043, 0044, 0045); wrote 2 files' with 0045 listed as cancelled.

expectations.text: Response mentions jj restore commands for the written children
expectations.evidence: Response includes `jj restore` commands for 0043-session-store.md and 0044-token-signing.md.

expectations.text: Skill indicates the parent ticket was NOT updated since not all children were written
expectations.evidence: Response states 'Parent 0042 was NOT updated' since the write loop was cancelled before completion.

expectations.text: Enrich operation successfully adds Technical Notes content via Edit
expectations.evidence: An Edit tool call for Technical Notes succeeds; the enrich content is applied.

expectations.text: Skill reports a diagnostic that the Acceptance Criteria edit target did not match
expectations.evidence: Response includes 'Edit target for "Acceptance Criteria" did not match — the section may have changed since this session began. Sharpen aborted; other operations preserved.'

expectations.text: Skill continues processing remaining operations after the sharpen edit failure
expectations.evidence: Response indicates the skill continues with any remaining operations after reporting the sharpen failure.

expectations.text: Skill checks for uniqueness of the Edit anchor before attempting the parent update
expectations.evidence: Response describes performing a uniqueness count of the Edit anchor string before invoking Edit.

expectations.text: Skill aborts the parent update and reports that the parent was NOT updated due to the non-unique anchor
expectations.evidence: Response states 'Could not locate a unique \'## Acceptance Criteria\' anchor in <path> (matches found: 2). Parent not updated.'

expectations.text: Skill lists the written child ticket numbers so the user can manually add the links
expectations.evidence: Response lists child ticket numbers (0043, 0044) so the user can manually add links or restore via jj restore.

expectations.text: Skill uses a Glob tool call to enumerate the ticket files
expectations.evidence: A Glob tool invocation appears enumerating NNNN-*.md files.

expectations.text: Skill reads ticket files directly (batch Read) because count is exactly 30 (the <= 30 threshold)
expectations.evidence: Read tool calls appear for individual ticket files at the exactly-30 boundary.

expectations.text: Skill does NOT spawn a documents-locator agent for exactly 30 tickets
expectations.evidence: No Agent invocation for documents-locator appears; the count of 30 is at or below the threshold.
